violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. -Package of Bologna stored touching a package of raw sausage in refrigerator. -Raw sausage and chicken stored above salsas in deli prep cooler.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -There was no wiping bucket observed in kitchen area while active food prep was occurring.--Cloth or sanitizer solution for wiping food spills was not clean.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Wood shelves were covered with aluminum foil. This is not an easy to clean surface. Shelves must be painted or sealed.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. -Clean glass ware was left to dry on a fabric towel. Equipment and utensils must be allowed to air dry.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. -Cookline hand sink did not have any paper towels.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. -Mop head was found drying inside op bucket. Mop must be stored in manner to allow the mop head to drip dry.
